title: RayRoPE: Projective Ray Positional Encoding for Multi-View Attention
gist: Apple and CMU researchers introduce RayRoPE, a positional encoding for multi-view transformers that encodes patches by a predicted 3D point along their ray, achieves SE(3)-invariant attention, and reports a 15% relative LPIPS improvement on CO3D novel-view synthesis.

insights.claim: Existing absolute and relative positional encoding schemes fail to simultaneously deliver unique patch encoding, SE(3)-invariant attention with multi-frequency similarity, and adaptivity to underlying scene geometry for multi-view transformers.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Signals an unmet gap in 3D vision transformer design that Apple is targeting for on-device spatial computing and depth applications.
insights.quote: We find that prior (absolute or relative) encoding schemes for multi-view attention do not meet the above desiderata, and present RayRoPE to address this gap.
insights.locator: We study positional encodings for multi-view transformers

insights.claim: RayRoPE encodes a patch position using a predicted 3D point along its associated ray rather than the ray direction, yielding a geometry-aware rather than purely directional encoding.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: The point-along-ray formulation is the mechanistic edge that lets attention adapt to scene depth, relevant for depth and reconstruction models.
insights.quote: RayRoPE represents patch positions based on associated rays but leverages a predicted point along the ray instead of the direction for a geometry-aware encoding.

insights.claim: SE(3) invariance is achieved by computing query-frame projective coordinates to derive multi-frequency similarity between patches across views.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: View-pose invariance is critical for robust multi-camera perception; the projective-coordinate trick is the reusable technique.
insights.quote: To achieve SE(3) invariance, RayRoPE computes query-frame projective coordinates for computing multi-frequency similarity.

insights.claim: Because the predicted 3D point along a ray may be imprecise, RayRoPE analytically computes the expected positional encoding under uncertainty rather than assuming a point estimate.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Baking uncertainty into the encoding is a differentiator that could make the method robust where depth predictions are noisy.
insights.quote: RayRoPE presents a mechanism to analytically compute the expected position encoding under uncertainty
insights.locator: as the ‘predicted’ 3D point along a ray may not be precise

insights.claim: RayRoPE delivers a 15% relative improvement on LPIPS on CO3D and consistently outperforms alternate positional encoding schemes on novel-view synthesis and stereo depth estimation.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A quantified double-digit perceptual-quality gain is the kind of benchmark lift that can shift adoption in 3D vision pipelines.
insights.quote: 15% relative improvement on LPIPS in CO3D
insights.locator: We validate RayRoPE on the tasks of novel-view synthesis

insights.claim: RayRoPE can positionally encode RGB-D input directly, producing even larger gains over baselines that cannot incorporate depth information into their encodings.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Native depth-sensor fusion favors device makers with LiDAR/depth hardware (e.g. Apple's sensor stack) for spatial AI.
insights.quote: RayRoPE can seamlessly incorporate RGB-D input, resulting in even larger gains over alternatives that cannot positionally encode this information.
insights.locator: We also show that RayRoPE can seamlessly incorporate RGB-D input
